title: Trust and Checkout Confidence
summary: Usability sessions exploring why shoppers hesitate at the final purchase screen even when pricing is acceptable. Hesitation was driven by missing trust signals: no visible security badges, an unfamiliar payment processor name, and no reassurance about refunds. Adding lightweight trust cues raised stated confidence to complete the purchase.

quotes.text: Everything looked fine until checkout, but I didn't recognize the payment company, so I paused and questioned whether it was safe.
quotes.speaker: Participant 2, mobile shopper

quotes.text: If I'd seen a refund guarantee right there, I would have just bought it instead of leaving to go research the brand.
quotes.speaker: Participant 7, comparison shopper
